prediction.attackType: T1505.003 Server Software Component: Web Shell
prediction.summary: An attacker successfully uploaded a web shell to the webroot of a server from IP 10.20.8.77. The web shell was then used to execute commands, including 'whoami', 'cat /etc/passwd', and an attempt to download a secondary payload from 'evil.tld'. Multiple security tools detected and alerted on this activity, confirming the presence and active use of a web shell.

timeline.event: Suspicious PHP file '/uploads/shell.php' uploaded to webroot.
WAF alerts on suspicious PHP upload to webroot /uploads/shell.php from 10.20.8.77.
Attempt to download 'stage2' from 'http://evil.tld/stage2' via web shell.
Web shell activity detected: repeated POST to .php with cmd parameter.
SIEM identifies MITRE T1505.003 Server Software Component: Web Shell.
ModSecurity rule 942100 SQLi probe blocked from 10.20.8.77 URI /uploads/shell.php.
SIEM issues CRITICAL alert for webshell activity on webserver01 from 10.20.8.77.

recommendations: Immediately isolate the compromised host (webserver01) to prevent further compromise or lateral movement.
Remove the uploaded web shell (/uploads/shell.php) and any other malicious files from the server.
Investigate the initial compromise vector to determine how the web shell was uploaded and patch the underlying vulnerability.
Review web server access logs and other relevant logs for any additional suspicious activity from source IP 10.20.8.77 or related indicators.
Analyze the downloaded 'stage2' payload (if available) and investigate 'evil.tld' for further threat intelligence.
Force password resets for any accounts potentially exposed by the 'cat /etc/passwd' command execution.
Enhance WAF/IDS rules and implement application-level controls to prevent similar file uploads and command execution attempts.
